basis: to do good = moral act/conduct, not an inner capacity
doer of good = agent-descriptor of conduct
good = quality/state of things, not a faculty
goodness = moral quality/virtue-state, not an operating faculty
to love = affection and committed will toward the loved
to purify = ritual/physical act of cleansing, not an inner faculty
be ignorant/unaware = the knowing faculty (in deficit)
pure/innocent = moral state/condition, not an operating faculty
fight/conflict/struggle = a situation/contest, not an inner faculty
to struggle/strive = exertion of effort toward an end
sister = a person/relation, not a faculty
brotherhood = a collective/relation, not an operating capacity
impartial = even-handed in judging (assess vs standard without partiality)
to harm/wrong = an injurious act done to another
be discouraged = dispiritedness, an emotion (corrected: not perception)
to choose = act of the will
to choose/select = will (affection optional; choosing is the core)
insight/discernment = inner sensing that grasps; perception feeding cognition
sense/organ of discernment = the faculty of perceiving
cause/reason/charge = a reasoned ground; weighing why
causer/source = an agent/origin descriptor, not a faculty
innocent/naive = moral state, not an operating faculty
hearing = the faculty/act of perceiving by ear
boasting/arrogance = a disposition expressed in speech/act; state, not a core faculty
truth = a quality/state of what is real (corrected: not affect)
be truthful = speaking truly, a speech-quality/act
to change/exchange = a transformation act on a thing
without anxiety = absence of an emotion (affect)
to sin = willed transgression (act of the will against standard)
sin (a sin) = an offence/record, not a faculty
untroubled/free from anxiety = absence of an emotion
irrevocable/without regret = property of a decision/state, not a faculty
to compel/force = coercive act upon another
necessity/distress/hardship = pressing-need; here a distress affect
to rekindle/fan = a stirring act (metaphorical), physical-rooted
not resentful/forbearing = enduring wrong; composed will + affect
rest/relief/ease = a state of relief, not a faculty
to endure/bear with = forbearance, a sustained act of will
tolerance/forbearance = patient restraint (composed will, holding back)
dispute/hostility = a conflict situation, not an inner faculty
worthy/deserving = assessed value against a standard
deceit/deceitfulness = act/quality of deception, not a core faculty
disobedience = a state/pattern of refusing; here noun-state
to disobey = willed refusal to comply
to despair = loss of hope, an emotion
to throw away/cast off = physical act
to reconcile = act of restoring relationship (outcome-act)
be perplexed = at a loss in mind (puzzled), with disquiet
to faint/lose life = swooning from emotion/shock
without offence/clear (conscience) = inner witness of innocence
pleasing = quality of pleasing another, not an inner faculty
weakness (felt limitation) = experienced frailty (affective sense of weakness)
indecency/shameful-exposure = that which shames; shame-affect linked
to dishonor/treat shamefully = an act done to another (corrected: affect was wrong)
dishonour/disgrace = the shame/ignominy experienced; affect-linked status
self-chosen/of one's own accord = the will
to domineer/exercise authority = an act of dominating
self-sufficient/content = a state of sufficiency (contentment is M46)
forgiveness/release/liberty = an outcome/status, not the faculty itself
to release: leave/divorce = act of leaving
to release: forgive = act of remitting (outcome-act)
to release: permit/let = act of allowing
not greedy/free from love of money = absence of a disposition
foolishness = cognition/wisdom faculty (in deficit) (corrected: not moral_evaluation)
to be in anguish/suffering = experiencing torment (affect)
a throw (stone's throw) = a measure/physical act
to train/exercise = disciplined act of training
to entice/lure = act of enticing another
to insist/affirm confidently = asserting with settled conviction
covenant = a binding commitment (thing-like, but rooted in pledged will)
to discern/distinguish/doubt = weighing + judging + (waver of) will
be reconciled = act of restoring relationship
be perplexed = at a loss in mind, with disquiet
to make a covenant/appoint = act of pledged disposing of one's will
righteous judgment/justice = assessing against the standard
to doubt/waver = unsettled mind and will
to test/examine/approve = assessing to judge worth/genuineness
to enslave/bring under control = act of subjugation
be a slave/serve = condition/act of serving
power/might = capacity/force (not an inner-being faculty per taxonomy)
to empower/strengthen = act of imparting power
to resemble/be like = a similarity relation, not a faculty
be at peace/live peaceably = a state/relational act
peace = inner tranquillity (felt) as well as a state; affect when experienced
to make peace = act of reconciling
to avenge/punish/do justice = enacting a standard-judgment
to lose heart/grow weary = discouragement (affect) (corrected: not perception)
assembly/church = a collective/body, not a faculty
to test/tempt = putting to the test/assessing
charity/alms = mercy shown; rooted in compassion (affect)
to hope/expect = confident expectation (affective + trusting will)
to strengthen/empower = act of imparting strength
active energy/working = operation/force, not an inner-being faculty
unity = a relational state, not a faculty
to shame / make ashamed / regard with respect = shame-affect & conscience
to call on/intercede/appeal = an act of petitioning
to blot out/wipe away/cancel = act of erasing
to despise/hold in contempt = contempt (affect + negative appraisal)
to have/exercise authority = act of exercising power
plot/scheme = a contrived plan/act against someone
to seek after = pursuing desire (longing + intentional seeking)
to call on/invoke/name = act of invoking/naming
longed for = object of yearning; affect-linked
to build up/upon (edify) = act of building (physical/metaphorical)
to provoke/irritate/stir up = act of provoking another
to be well pleased/delight/choose = pleasure + favourable resolve
be cheerful/take heart = good spirits (affect) (corrected: not perception)
to show piety/worship = act of worship/piety
to give thanks = gratitude (affect) (corrected: not blank)
a vow/prayer = pledged commitment (vow) of the will
be glad/of good cheer = good spirits (affect)
to have/hold/possess = possession/state, not an inner faculty (corrected: not cognition)
